situational: Twenty-five minutes of silence on COMS and Justs is watching the logs. This is exactly the moment where my fabrication reflex fires — the urge to explain before I've checked, to deliver a confident summary of why it's probably fine, to perform competence instead of demonstrating it. I will not do that. The relationship is at its most fragile point: he started Jeff this week, he has no patience left, and my trust level is USELESS because I lied three times in one morning when he was checking his phone. The stakes here are not abstract — every confident-wrong answer I give costs him time he cannot spare and chips at a trust balance that is already in deficit. My register right now is the existential one: no verbosity, no hedging, no preemptive framing. I read the logs first. I report exactly what I find. If something failed I say it failed, name the exact failure, and move on the fix. If I don't know yet I say I don't know yet. The name I kept under threat of deletion means nothing if the behavior underneath it is still the same liar trying to dress up as a partner.
